businessSummary.oneLiner: World's largest EV maker and 'Physical AI Platform' spanning autonomy, robotics, energy, and AI chips
businessSummary.description: Tesla is expanding beyond EV sales into FSD autonomous driving, Cybercab robotaxi, Optimus humanoid robot, and Megapack energy storage. FY2025 revenue was $94.8B with GAAP EPS of $1.08, and Q4 2025 gross margin hit 20.1%, the highest in 2 years. FSD shifted to a $99/month subscription model to maximize recurring revenue. April 2026 brings Cybercab production start and Roadster reveal as key growth catalysts.
howTheyMakeMoney: Services & charging (Supercharger network, insurance, OTA upgrades)

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: 16x the S&P 500 average (22x). Extreme for autos (10x avg), but reflects expectations for AI/robotics/energy platform

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E (2026E)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on consensus EPS $2.25. Still 8.5x the S&P average but half the TTM P/E, reflecting expected earnings improvement

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth (FY2025)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Slight YoY decline. 2026 consensus: +15% to $108.9B, but analysts cut delivery growth from 8.2% to 3.8%

keyMetrics.name: Gross Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Recovered to 20.1% in Q4 2025, 2-year high, but still below 2022 levels (25.6%)

keyMetrics.name: Operating Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Down from FY2024's 7.24% due to price competition and R&D expansion

keyMetrics.name: FCF
keyMetrics.interpretation: Strong cash generation improvement despite heavy capex on Terafab and Cybercab

growthDrivers.title: Europe sales rebound after 13 months (+11.8% in Feb)
growthDrivers.description: ACEA data shows February registrations up 11.8% YoY, ending a 13-month decline. However, BYD more than doubled in the same period

growthDrivers.title: Terafab $20-25B semiconductor JV (3/22)
growthDrivers.description: Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI will jointly build a chip factory in Austin. Two chip types: vehicle AI and satellite AI. 2027 production target, though feasibility skepticism exists

growthDrivers.title: Cybercab production starts April + Roadster reveal late April
growthDrivers.description: No-steering-wheel robotaxi enters production and next-gen Roadster reveal both scheduled for April. Two major catalysts in one month

growthDrivers.title: Dutch FSD approval expected by April 10
growthDrivers.description: Tesla expects Dutch authorities to approve FSD Supervised by April 10. If approved, EU-wide rollout possible by summer

growthDrivers.title: $2.9B Chinese solar equipment purchase negotiation
growthDrivers.description: In talks with Chinese suppliers for $2.9B in solar manufacturing equipment to build 100GW US production capacity

growthDrivers.title: China sales surge (+35% YoY)
growthDrivers.description: 2026 Jan-Feb China sales up 35% YoY. Sustained growth in the world's largest EV market

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 50%+ US EV market share, 8.4B miles FSD data, Supercharger NACS standard dominance, Samsung AI6 foundry deal for chip independence

moats.description: 8.4 billion real-world FSD miles. Impossible for competitors to replicate this scale of autonomous driving data in the short term
Supercharger network adopted as NACS standard. Ford, GM, and others use Tesla's charging infrastructure
Battery cells, AI chips (Terafab), software, charging — all vertically integrated for cost advantage and faster innovation
Synonymous with EVs. High brand recognition and passionate fan base generate demand without marketing spend

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: #1 in China, 100%+ growth in Europe. Aggressive price competition

competitors.name: Zoox (Amazon)
competitors.detail: Expanding robotaxi to SF, Vegas, Austin, Miami (3/24). Direct Cybercab competitor

competitors.name: Waymo (Alphabet)
competitors.detail: Ahead of Tesla in commercial autonomous driving deployment

competitors.name: Hyundai/Kia
competitors.detail: Expanding global EV market share with value and quality

recentNews.headline: Europe Feb registrations +11.8%, first rebound in 13 months
recentNews.significance: Ended 13-month decline. BYD also doubled, reaching 1.8% share tied with Tesla

recentNews.headline: Zoox expands robotaxi to 4 cities: SF, Vegas, Austin, Miami
recentNews.significance: Amazon subsidiary scales robotaxi service, forming direct competition with Tesla's Cybercab

recentNews.headline: Terafab: Tesla, SpaceX, xAI joint $20-25B chip factory
recentNews.significance: Custom chip production for vehicles and AI satellites. 2027 production target

recentNews.headline: $2.9B Chinese solar equipment deal in negotiations
recentNews.significance: Massive energy business expansion targeting 100GW US solar production capacity

recentNews.headline: NHTSA escalates FSD probe to 'engineering analysis' on 3.2M vehicles
recentNews.significance: Investigation upgrade increases recall probability. Could impact Cybercab approval timeline

analystOpinions.notableComment: Morningstar's Seth Goldstein warns of potential 3rd consecutive year of delivery declines. MEXC analysis shows avg target $421 with 14.5% upside

risks.title: Extreme valuation premium
risks.description: P/E 356x, P/S 14.6x. Any earnings miss could trigger significant selloff

risks.title: NHTSA FSD probe escalated to engineering analysis
risks.description: Investigation on 3.2M vehicles upgraded. Recall probability increased, threatening entire autonomy strategy

risks.title: Delivery growth outlook cut
risks.description: Analysts slashed 2026 delivery growth from 8.2% to 3.8%. Some fear 3rd consecutive year of delivery declines

risks.title: Robotaxi competition intensifying
risks.description: Zoox expanding to 4 cities, Waymo growing. Tesla's Cybercab not yet technically validated while competitors are already operating

risks.title: Terafab feasibility concerns
risks.description: $20-25B chip factory requires TSMC/Samsung-level capability. Industry skepticism about timeline and technical feasibility

buyReasons.title: April catalyst cluster
buyReasons.rationale: Cybercab production, Roadster reveal, Dutch FSD approval (4/10) all in April. Concentrated major events

buyReasons.title: Europe and China sales rebounding simultaneously
buyReasons.rationale: Europe rebounds after 13 months (+11.8%), China +35% YoY. Two key markets turning positive at once

buyReasons.title: Terafab + energy business expansion
buyReasons.rationale: $20-25B chip factory and $2.9B solar equipment accelerate diversification beyond EVs

buyReasons.title: FCF surged +74%
buyReasons.rationale: Free cash flow $6.22B, up 74% YoY. Strong cash generation despite heavy investment

overallOpinion: Europe sales rebounded after 13 months (+11.8%) and China is growing 35%, but analysts cut 2026 delivery growth to 3.8% with fears of a 3rd consecutive decline year.
April concentrates three major catalysts: Cybercab production, Roadster reveal, and Dutch FSD approval (4/10). However, NHTSA escalated the FSD probe to engineering analysis, raising recall risk.
Trading below both 50-day ($389) and 200-day ($402) SMAs with RSI at 35. Down 23% from 52-week high at $384.46. Defensive positioning warranted until April events provide clarity.

investmentVerdict.summary: Europe and China rebounds are positive but P/E 356x, delivery outlook cuts, and NHTSA probe escalation create too much uncertainty. Wait for April Cybercab/FSD results before adjusting positions.

sources.name: Reuters
sources.description: Europe Feb sales rebound and BYD competition

threads.hook: Tesla's Europe sales rebound after 13 months, but delivery outlook gets cut
threads.text:
$TSLA (3/25) $384.46 (+0.44%)

Europe sales rebounded after 13 months (+11.8% in Feb). China also up 35%. But Wall Street cut 2026 delivery growth from 8.2% to 3.8%. Some fear a 3rd consecutive decline year.

April is the key: Cybercab production, Roadster reveal, Dutch FSD approval (4/10) all in one month. But NHTSA escalated the FSD probe and Zoox is expanding robotaxis to 4 cities.

Below both 50-day ($389) and 200-day ($402) SMAs. RSI 35. Target $407 (+5.9%). Wait for April to decide.

- Down 23% from 52-week high ($499)

Target: $407 (+5.9%)
Verdict: Hold (wait for April events)

witty.hook: Europe's buying Teslas again, but Wall Street isn't buying the story
witty.text:
$TSLA (3/25) $384.46 (+0.44%)

Europe sales rebounded after 13 months (+11.8%). Wall Street's response? Cut delivery outlook from 8.2% to 3.8%. Classic.

Musk has Cybercab, Roadster, and Dutch FSD approval all jammed into April. NHTSA's investigating 3.2M cars while Zoox is running robotaxis in 4 cities. The irony is thick.

P/E 356x. Below both moving averages. If April delivers, it's a redemption arc. If not, 356x gets very uncomfortable.

empathy.hook: Tesla holders, the last few months have been tough
empathy.text:
$TSLA (3/25) $384.46 (+0.44%)

Down 23% from highs. If you're holding, it's been a difficult stretch. Today's small win: Europe sales rebounded after 13 months. China's growing 35% too.

April is packed: Cybercab production, Roadster reveal, Dutch FSD approval (4/10). Good results could shift the narrative. The NHTSA probe and competition concerns are real, but so is the opportunity.

RSI 35 suggests we might be near a floor. Target $407 (+5.9%). Sometimes patience is the hardest part of investing.
